title: GameChanger protocol overview
description: A guided walk through the core concepts of the GameChanger protocol: who the actors are, how a script flows from issuer to wallet to backend, and where the trust boundaries sit.

stops.title: The three actors
stops.narrative:
There are three parties in any GameChanger integration: an [Issuer](node:backend) (typically a Haskell backend) that builds scripts, a [Wallet](node:wallet) that holds keys and performs signing, and a [User](node:user) who confirms the operation in the wallet UI.

Notice that only the wallet touches keys. Everything else in this graph — issuer, backend, signed result, callback — sees data, never secrets.

stops.title: The script is the protocol
stops.narrative:
The [script](node:script-sign-tx) is a JSON document declaring two things: what actions to run and where their results should go.

Here the script has two [actions](node:action-build-tx) — [buildTx](node:action-build-tx) and [signTx](node:action-sign-tx) — plus a single [export](node:export-signed).

stops.title: Script becomes URL
stops.narrative: The backend encodes the script into a [resolver URL](node:resolver-url) (JSON → gzip → base64url). The user's browser opens that URL. The script's content is fully visible in the URL — treat it as public data.

stops.title: Session binding
stops.narrative: Before issuing the URL, the backend [mints](edge:gc:mints) a per-request [Session](node:session-t1) and binds it to the resolver URL. When the callback arrives, the backend checks the session is valid, single-use, and unexpired. Without this, callbacks are unauthenticated.

stops.title: Result via the post channel
stops.narrative: The script's [export](node:export-signed) uses the [post channel](node:channel-post): a browser-side HTTP POST to the backend's callback endpoint. This is the only way a non-browser process observes the signature.

stops.title: Attacks and mitigations
stops.narrative: Three attack vectors bound to this topology: [callback spoofing](node:attack-spoof-callback), [replay of the signed tx](node:attack-replay-tx), and [phishing via a crafted URL](node:attack-phish-url). Each is [mitigated](edge:gc:mitigates) by a specific control: session binding, on-chain presence checks, and the wallet's in-UI confirmation.
